explanation: Performing integer division before multiplication (a / b * c) truncates the intermediate result, so precision is lost before the final value is computed. Solidity integer division always rounds toward zero, and any remainder discarded by the early division can never be recovered by the later multiplication.
impact: Rewards, fees, or share calculations come out systematically too low (sometimes zero), letting an attacker exploit the rounding gap to underpay fees, extract value from vaults, or skew accounting in their favor.
